verdicts.issue: IV magnesium sulfate is correct next step per RCH Asthma Acute when continuous nebulised bronchodilators and IV steroid have failed; sources cited correctly.
Conjugated hyperbilirubinaemia >20% of total with pale stools mandates urgent fasting abdominal ultrasound to exclude biliary atresia; matches RCH Jaundice in early infancy.
Staggered/unknown-time paracetamol ingestion above toxic threshold warrants empirical IV acetylcysteine without waiting for nomogram; matches RCH Paracetamol poisoning and TG Toxicology.

Metaphyseal corner fracture in a non-ambulant infant with torso bruising in varying stages is highly specific for NAI; skeletal survey + child protection notification is standard; matches RCH Child Abuse.
Fluid-refractory paediatric septic shock (post 40 mL/kg) with cold shock features warrants peripheral adrenaline infusion; matches RCH Sepsis.
Moderate bronchiolitis with feeds <50% baseline warrants admission for NG hydration plus minimal handling per RCH Bronchiolitis.
Supportive care (suction, oxygen prn, fluids) is standard for typical bronchiolitis; lead-in retains markdown bold which is consistent with rest of bank.
Acute neuro deterioration during DKA treatment warrants empirical hyperosmolar therapy (mannitol or hypertonic saline) and senior escalation before confirmatory imaging; matches RCH DKA and ISPAD.
Classic intussusception triad (colicky pain, lethargy, red mucus); ultrasound is first-line per RCH Intussusception.
Febrile neutropenia (neutrophils 0.1) mandates blood cultures + immediate empirical IV antibiotics; matches RCH Fever and suspected or confirmed neutropenia.
Severe DKA in 13yo who missed insulin during viral illness warrants 0.9% NaCl bolus then paediatric DKA protocol (insulin started 1-2h after fluids); matches RCH DKA.
Moderate-to-severe bronchiolitis with persistent hypoxia and WOB on low-flow O2 warrants escalation to high-flow nasal prong therapy plus continued supportive care; matches RCH Bronchiolitis.
Suspected meningococcal septic shock warrants immediate IV ceftriaxone plus aggressive resuscitation; LP and CT deferred. Matches RCH Meningococcal Infection, ANZCOR Paediatric Sepsis.
Severe acute asthma with rising pCO2 (56), fatigue, and reduced air entry indicates impending respiratory failure requiring PICU escalation; matches RCH Acute Asthma and ANZCOR.
Febrile 7-week-old infant with lethargy and positive nitrites/LE on catheter urine warrants empirical IV antibiotics + full septic evaluation + admission; matches RCH Febrile Child and TG Antibiotic.
Suspected bacterial meningitis with sepsis features warrants blood cultures + immediate empirical IV antibiotics over LP-first; matches RCH Meningitis.
Unexplained upper-arm bruise with reduced limb use mandates XR + documentation and consideration of NAI; matches RCH Child Abuse and RCH Pulled Elbow.
Classic intussusception with palpable sausage-shaped RUQ mass and red currant jelly stool warrants surgical consult + air enema after stabilisation; matches RCH Intussusception.
Day 6 of fever with classic Kawasaki criteria (conjunctivitis, mucosal change, extremity oedema, rash, lymphadenopathy) warrants IVIG + aspirin + echo; matches RCH Kawasaki Disease.
Symptomatic severe hyponatraemia with seizure (Na 119) is an indication for 3% hypertonic saline; matches RCH Hyponatraemia.
Renal involvement (haematuria, proteinuria, hypertension) is the strongest predictor of long-term morbidity in IgA vasculitis and warrants closer inpatient monitoring; matches RCH HSP.
Sudden severe scrotal pain with high-riding testis and absent cremasteric reflex mandates immediate surgical exploration without delay for imaging; matches RCH Acute Scrotal Pain.
Cushing-type physiology (bradycardia, hypertension) with reduced GCS during DKA treatment is classic for cerebral oedema; immediate hyperosmolar therapy + ICU escalation; matches RCH DKA and ISPAD.
Bronchiolitis with hypoxia (SpO2 90%) and feeding 50% warrants supportive care with O2 + feeding assessment; matches RCH Bronchiolitis.

Bronchiolitis with SpO2 91% + moderate WOB + feeds 50% meets admission criteria; supportive care (O2, small frequent feeds) is correct; matches RCH Bronchiolitis. Distractor B/D/E plausibly wrong with reasoning aligned to current evidence.

Febrile 18-day-old neonate warrants full septic workup including LP plus empirical IV ampicillin + gentamicin per RCH Febrile Child and TG Neonatal Sepsis; ampicillin q12h dosing is appropriate for term neonate 8-28 days.

Acute severe asthma (SpO2 90, single-word speech) warrants O2 to target SpO2 >=94%, neb salbutamol 5 mg + ipratropium 250 mcg q20min for first hour, plus oral prednisolone 1 mg/kg; matches Australian Asthma Handbook and RCH Acute Asthma.
Mild-moderate croup with stridor at rest but no significant recession or hypoxia warrants single oral dexamethasone 0.15 mg/kg with 2-4h observation; matches RCH Croup.
Moderate gastro dehydration (~5%) with failed oral intake warrants NG ORS 50 mL/kg over 4h plus ondansetron; matches RCH Gastroenteritis.
Outpatient CAP in immunised child warrants oral amoxicillin 25 mg/kg TDS x 5 days; matches TG Respiratory and RCH antimicrobial guidelines.
Mild croup warrants single oral dexamethasone 0.15 mg/kg; matches RCH Croup.
Moderate bronchiolitis (SpO2 90%, feeds halved) warrants admission for O2 to maintain SpO2 >=90% plus NG fluids; matches RCH Bronchiolitis.
Simple febrile convulsion (generalised, <15 min, fully recovered, no neuro deficit) warrants reassurance + parental education only; matches RCH Febrile Convulsion.

Kawasaki disease meeting criteria warrants IVIG 2 g/kg single dose plus high-dose aspirin 30-50 mg/kg/day acute phase then low-dose; matches RCH Kawasaki and AHA; note Australian centres are increasingly using lower-dose aspirin (3-5 mg/kg) from outset but high-dose remains a defensible Australian option.
Intussusception suspected on classic clinical picture: ultrasound is first-line investigation per RCH Intussusception.
Childhood nephrotic syndrome (age 3, classic features, normal BP, no haematuria) warrants empirical prednisolone (ISKDC regimen) without upfront biopsy; matches RCH Nephrotic Syndrome.
Paediatric DKA with ~10-14% deficit warrants 10 mL/kg 0.9% NaCl bolus then deficit replacement evenly over 48h with 0.9% NaCl + K once UOP confirmed; matches current RCH DKA and ISPAD 2022.

Meningococcal septicaemia with shock and purpura warrants immediate IV ceftriaxone 100 mg/kg (max 2 g) plus aggressive 0.9% NaCl resuscitation; LP and CT deferred until stabilised. Matches RCH Meningococcal and TG Meningitis.
High-risk BRUE (age <60 days, preterm, tactile-stim required) warrants admission for at least 24h cardiorespiratory monitoring plus ECG; matches RCH BRUE and AAP 2016 BRUE guideline.
Post-nebulised-adrenaline observation period for croup is minimum 4 hours due to 2-4h duration of action and risk of rebound; matches RCH Croup.
Febrile 6-week-old warrants full septic workup including LP plus empirical IV ampicillin + gentamicin per RCH Febrile Child.

Severe acute asthma unresponsive to maximal inhaled bronchodilator therapy plus oral steroid warrants IV magnesium sulphate 50 mg/kg (max 2 g) over 20 minutes as second-line; matches Australian Asthma Handbook and RCH Acute Asthma.

Phototherapy threshold value of ~260 micromol/L at 72 hours for a term low-risk infant appears too low. The current RCH/NICE-aligned nomogram for term infants >=38 weeks without risk factors has a phototherapy threshold closer to 320-350 micromol/L at 72 hours; the Queensland Clinical Guidelines / RCH nomogram value at 72h for low-risk term is approximately 340 micromol/L. The correct answer (option C with 260) is therefore biochemically wrong - a TSB of 265 at 72h in a term low-risk infant would NOT warrant phototherapy on current Australian nomograms. Recommend dropping or rewriting the entire question with verified threshold values from the current RCH nomogram, since the entire question hinges on a numerical threshold that is incorrect.

fix.verdict_note: Cannot fix in place because the threshold value drives the correct-answer choice; the option text, rationale, and explanation all rest on a wrong numeric anchor. Recommend rewriting with correct thresholds OR dropping. Suggested replacement: change TSB to a value clearly above the 320-340 threshold (e.g., 360 micromol/L) and align option C threshold to ~340 micromol/L. Until rewritten, mark as drop.

verdicts.issue: Asymptomatic term infant with maternal intrapartum fever 38.2 and ROM 20h with incomplete IAP warrants blood culture + CRP + empirical IV benzylpenicillin + gentamicin until cultures negative; matches RCH Early-Onset Neonatal Sepsis.
HSP with nephrotic-range proteinuria (UPCR 250 mg/mmol) warrants paediatric nephrology referral and consideration of renal biopsy to guide immunosuppression; matches RCH HSP and KDIGO.
CF DIOS managed with oral Gastrografin plus IV fluids and NG decompression if vomiting; matches RCH CF GI and CF Foundation guidelines.

Recurrent AOM in Aboriginal child warrants amoxicillin-clavulanate 22.5 mg/kg BD x 7 days per Otitis Media Guidelines for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children; phrasing uses full term not the banned abbreviation. Note: a Y4 student might reasonably pick amoxicillin x 7 days as the more common cited Aboriginal-paediatric AOM regimen - both 7-day amoxicillin and 7-day amoxicillin-clavulanate are defensible per the OM guidelines (the latter favoured for otitis-prone or recent amox use); the recurrent AOM history in the stem justifies the clavulanate.

Severe infant pertussis with extreme leukocytosis (WCC 45 with lymphocyte predominance) warrants exchange transfusion or leukapheresis to prevent pulmonary hypertension; matches RCH Pertussis and published infant pertussis literature.
AOM diagnosis based on bulging immobile erythematous TM is correct per TG AOM; very easy question (difficulty 2) but factually sound.
Neonatal BGL 2.2 mmol/L with no clinical signs warrants feeding plus recheck and buccal dextrose 40% gel 200 mg/kg if BGL stays <2.6; matches Queensland Clinical Guideline.
Poorly controlled paediatric asthma on low-dose ICS (waking >=2 nights/week, reliever >=4-5 times/week) warrants step-up to moderate-dose ICS per Australian Asthma Handbook.

Short stature + delayed puberty + webbed neck + low hairline + broad chest with widely spaced nipples is classic Turner syndrome; diagnosis straightforward, distractors plausible (Noonan, GH deficiency).

Paediatric OSA with kissing tonsils and classic symptoms can be referred directly to ENT for adenotonsillectomy per RCH OSA; polysomnography is alternative defensible answer in absence of clear hypoxia, but ENT referral is acceptable given grade 4 tonsils and witnessed apnoeas. Distractor B (PSG) is genuinely plausible.
Neonatal abstinence syndrome with Finnegan score 12 warrants pharmacological treatment with oral morphine; naloxone is contraindicated in opioid-dependent neonate; matches RCH NAS.
Impetiginised eczema with pustules (no vesicles) warrants oral cephalexin plus wet dressings with diluted topical steroid; matches RCH Atopic Dermatitis and TG Atopic Dermatitis.
ANA-positive oligoarticular JIA in young girl mandates regular slit-lamp screening for asymptomatic chronic anterior uveitis; matches RCH JIA.
Perianal fistula + skip lesions + terminal ileal involvement is diagnostic of Crohn's disease over UC; matches RCH IBD.
Pancytopenia + hepatosplenomegaly + blast cells on film + bone pain is classic ALL; matches RCH Acute Leukaemia.
ASD screening at 18-24 months with M-CHAT-R/F is correct per RCH ASD.
Stable new T1DM without DKA can start subcutaneous basal-bolus insulin with diabetes team support; matches RCH Type 1 Diabetes New Diagnosis.
Persistent anaphylaxis after first IM adrenaline dose warrants repeat IM adrenaline every 5-15 minutes; matches ASCIA and RCH Anaphylaxis. Dose 0.01 mg/kg IM is correct (7yo ~25kg = 0.15 mg).

Moderate bronchiolitis with SpO2 90% + feeds halved + moderate WOB meets admission criteria for supportive care (O2, hydration); matches RCH Bronchiolitis. Note: this is near-duplicate of paeds-batch-001 and paeds-batch-008.
Pyloric stenosis with hypochloraemic alkalosis requires isotonic 0.9% NaCl bolus then 0.9% NaCl + 5% dextrose at maintenance; potassium added only after UOP confirmed; matches RCH Pyloric Stenosis.
Poor asthma control on low-dose ICS warrants step-up to moderate-dose ICS; matches Australian Asthma Handbook. Near-duplicate of paeds-batch-036.

Asymptomatic infant with anti-Ro-mediated complete heart block and adequate ventricular escape rhythm warrants close monitoring + paediatric cardiology referral for possible pacemaker; matches RCH Neonatal Lupus.
Giant coronary aneurysm (>=8 mm or z-score >=10) post-Kawasaki warrants warfarin (target INR 2-3) plus low-dose aspirin per AHA Kawasaki Disease Guideline.

Febrile UTI in 3-month-old warrants admission + IV ampicillin + gentamicin + renal ultrasound; matches RCH UTI. Note: ceftriaxone is also acceptable in some Australian centres for older infants but amp+gent is appropriate at 3 months.
Water deprivation test response: failed concentration with water deprivation but adequate concentration after exogenous desmopressin is classic central DI; matches RCH DI.
HSP with nephrotic-range proteinuria warrants paediatric nephrology referral and possible biopsy; matches RCH HSP. Near-duplicate of paeds-batch-030.
Stable confirmed intussusception without peritonism/perforation warrants air enema reduction; matches RCH Intussusception.
CF DIOS managed with oral Gastrografin + IV fluids + NG decompression; matches RCH CF GI. Near-duplicate of paeds-batch-031.
Severe infant pertussis WCC 52 with cyanotic paroxysms warrants exchange transfusion; matches RCH Pertussis. Near-duplicate of paeds-batch-033.

Correct answer (NAC infusion) is right and well-supported, but the rationale incorrectly states the Rumack-Matthew treatment line is 'approximately 1500 micromol/L at 10 hours'. The Australian/Rumack-Matthew nomogram treatment line at 10 hours post-ingestion is approximately 500-650 micromol/L (~75-100 microgram/mL), not 1500. A level of 1800 micromol/L at 9 hours is hugely above the treatment line, not just above it. Suggest rewording the rationale.

fix.options[0].rationale: The paracetamol level at 9 hours is 1800 micromol/L, well above the Rumack-Matthew nomogram treatment line (approximately 500 micromol/L at 10 hours post-ingestion in Australian guidelines). NAC is indicated and must be started immediately to prevent hepatotoxicity. The standard IV NAC protocol in Australia is the three-bag regimen. (Source: RCH CPG: Paracetamol Poisoning; Australian Therapeutic Guidelines: Toxicology)
fix.explanation.key_points[0]: Administer NAC if paracetamol level is above the treatment line on the Australian nomogram (e.g., approximately 1000 micromol/L at 4 hours, dropping to approximately 500 micromol/L at 10 hours). (Source: RCH CPG: Paracetamol Poisoning)

verdicts.issue: Severe acute asthma unresponsive to inhaled bronchodilators and oral steroid warrants IV magnesium sulphate 50 mg/kg over 20 min; matches Australian Asthma Handbook. Near-duplicate of paeds-batch-018.
Term infant with moderate HIE and abnormal aEEG warrants therapeutic hypothermia to 33.5C for 72 hours followed by controlled rewarming; matches RCH Neonatal Encephalopathy, TOBY trial, ILCOR.
Perianal fistulising Crohn's warrants anti-TNF (infliximab) plus immunomodulator in addition to antibiotics and surgical drainage; matches RCH IBD and ECCO/ESPGHAN.

Subtopic field says 'Neonatal hyperammonaemia' but the stem describes an 18-month-old; the clinical management (nitrogen scavengers + arginine) is correct for any age with urea cycle defect but the subtopic mismatch is a minor metadata issue. Otherwise, content is correct.

Refractory status epilepticus failing buccal midazolam + IV levetiracetam warrants midazolam infusion (or phenobarbitone), with thiopentone as anaesthetic agent if needed; matches RCH Status Epilepticus.
Low complement C3 in nephrotic syndrome raises suspicion of MPGN, PSGN, or lupus nephritis - all warranting biopsy before empirical steroids; matches RCH Nephrotic Syndrome and KDIGO.
Pyloric stenosis pre-op metabolic correction targets Cl >95 mmol/L and HCO3 <30 mmol/L to minimise perioperative apnoea risk; matches RCH Pyloric Stenosis.
Term newborn with HR >100 post-PPV but persistent cyanosis warrants titration of supplemental O2 to preductal SpO2 target; routine tracheal suction for meconium no longer recommended per ANZCOR 13.4.

DKA cerebral oedema with headache, bradycardia, hypertension, GCS drop warrants immediate mannitol 0.5-1 g/kg OR 3% hypertonic saline 5 mL/kg plus urgent CT; matches RCH DKA and ISPAD. Near-duplicate of both-batch-finals-002 and both-finals-117.
SMA syndrome with classic CT angiography findings (aortomesenteric angle 6 degrees) in adolescent with severe weight loss; clinical context matches.
Biliary atresia prognosis pivots on Kasai timing before 60 days of age; matches RCH Biliary Atresia.
Severe acute chest syndrome with respiratory failure and rising HbS load warrants exchange transfusion to lower HbS <30%; matches RCH Sickle Cell and NHLBI.
Severe paediatric TBI with herniation signs and hypotension warrants hypertonic saline (preferred over mannitol in shock as volume expander); matches RCH TBI and BTF paediatric guidelines.

Loculated empyema not draining after chest drain warrants intrapleural fibrinolytic (urokinase or alteplase); matches BTS and RCH Empyema. Note: 'Source: NCPC Guidelines' citation in distractor C is acceptable; alteplase often preferred over urokinase in current Australian practice.

Methylene blue for refractory anaphylactic shock is published in critical care literature but is not a standard Australian Y4-level answer and is not the first-line rescue agent in ASCIA guidance. ASCIA-aligned next steps for refractory anaphylactic shock after maximised adrenaline infusion and fluid resuscitation are: add glucagon (if beta-blocker), add a second vasopressor (vasopressin or noradrenaline), and consider extracorporeal life support. Methylene blue is a niche rescue and probably not the best 'single most appropriate' for a Y4 cohort. Recommend rewriting the question so the lead-in points to an appropriate Australian-guideline-aligned next step (e.g., adding a second vasopressor or escalating to ECMO consultation), OR retaining as a difficulty 5 question but rewording the lead-in to 'most appropriate rescue therapy in vasoplegic anaphylactic shock unresponsive to adrenaline'.

fix.options[3].rationale: Adding a second vasopressor such as vasopressin or noradrenaline is recommended in refractory anaphylactic shock when adrenaline infusion and fluid resuscitation have not restored perfusion; vasopressin counters the vasoplegia from histamine release. (Source: ASCIA Anaphylaxis Guidelines; ANZCOR Anaphylaxis)
fix.options[0].rationale: Methylene blue has been used in case reports of refractory anaphylactic shock but is not a first-line rescue agent in ASCIA or ANZCOR Australian guidance; vasopressin or noradrenaline are preferred. (Source: ASCIA Anaphylaxis)
fix.explanation.summary: Refractory anaphylactic shock not responding to maximised adrenaline infusion and aggressive fluid resuscitation warrants addition of a second vasopressor (vasopressin or noradrenaline) while continuing adrenaline. Glucagon is for patients on beta-blockers. Methylene blue is a published rescue option but is not first-line Australian therapy. ECMO should be considered if pharmacological measures fail. (Source: ASCIA; ANZCOR)

verdicts.issue: Oesophageal button battery in symptomatic 2yo with double-ring sign warrants urgent endoscopy within 2 hours plus honey or sucralfate while awaiting; matches RCH Button Battery and NCPC.

MIS-C diagnosis (fever, mucocutaneous features, cardiac dysfunction, positive SARS-CoV-2 IgG, hyperinflammation with lymphopenia + thrombocytopenia) warrants IVIG 2 g/kg + methylprednisolone + haemodynamic support; matches RCH PIMS-TS and WHO.

Immunogenic loss of response (high anti-infliximab antibodies + subtherapeutic trough) warrants switch within class (to adalimumab) plus consideration of immunomodulator; matches ECCO/ESPGHAN TDM guidelines.

Anti-NMDAR encephalitis with ovarian teratoma warrants immediate immunotherapy (high-dose methylprednisolone + IVIG) plus urgent oophorectomy; matches RCH Autoimmune Encephalitis and Titulaer Lancet Neurol 2013.

Acute liver failure with Coombs-negative haemolytic anaemia, low ALP and AST:ALT >2 is classic for Wilson disease; diagnostic workup of ceruloplasmin, serum copper, and 24h urinary copper is correct per EASL.
